summary:
The main branches of the external carotid artery are the superior thyroid, ascending pharyngeal, lingual, facial, superficial temporal, posterior auricular, and occipital arteries; however, neither the maxillary nor the ophthalmic arteries originate from it but rather from other sources such as the internal carotid artery.
```

The summary focuses only on presenting two core facts derived primarily from Agent1’s detailed analysis without repeating unnecessary details:

- Key branching patterns of the external carotid artery,
- Clarification regarding which options do NOT arise from its direct supply due to their origin being elsewhere within different arterial systems like those supplying head structures indirectly by way of deeper pathways starting with more proximal origins including both common and internal carotids. ```plaintext Summary: 
Key branches of the external carotid artery include the superior thyroid, ascending pharyngeal, lingual, facial, superficial temporal, posterior auricular, and occipital arteries. Neither the maxillary nor the ophthalmic arteries come directly from these branches—they instead stem from alternative routes involving the internal carotid system.
```
